stimulus: Because a parabolic mirror is curved along a single axis, light striking it from a faraway point converges at one focal point. A spherical mirror, ______blank can be likened to the inside of a polished bowl: it produces a blurred band of light rather than a single sharp point, since rays striking its edges focus differently from those striking its center.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. This sentence compares a spherical mirror to a parabolic one. It describes how a spherical mirror produces a blurred band of light rather than the single sharp focal point produced by a parabolic mirror. So the transition "by contrast" fits the context perfectly.

Choice B is incorrect. "In other words" indicates a restatement of the same idea in different words, but this sentence doesn't restate the previous one—it introduces a different kind of mirror with different behavior. Choice C is incorrect. "Likewise" indicates the addition of an agreeing idea, but a spherical mirror behaves differently from a parabolic one, not similarly. Choice D is incorrect. "For example" introduces an example, but a spherical mirror is not an example of a parabolic mirror; it is a different type that behaves differently.
